findings: 发现 3 个新的 install/hook/skill source drift：Cursor 活跃 bootstrap 副本仍是旧长版规则；fabric-archive gate ref 与测试仍验证 retired archive-hint 名；store-backed 写路径已生效但 archive/review/import skill 热路径仍指向本地 .fabric/knowledge/pending。

issues_found.title: Active Cursor bootstrap managed block is stale relative to canonical store-only bootstrap
issues_found.description: The checked-in active Cursor rule still contains the old long-form bootstrap block with project-local `.fabric/knowledge/pending/`, `fabric doctor --fix`, self-archive marker, optional `KB:` cite-line, and cite-coverage details. Current root AGENTS/canonical bootstrap says knowledge may only be written to mounted stores and pending entries go to the current write store. Install tests also assert Cursor's managed block body should byte-equal `BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL`, so the repository's generated Cursor copy is drifted and will instruct Cursor users differently from Codex/AGENTS.
issues_found.fix_direction: Regenerate or replace `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` from `BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL`, and add a repo-level drift check for checked-in generated client bootstrap copies.

issues_found.title: fabric-archive trigger-gate docs and tests still validate retired `archive-hint.cjs`
issues_found.description: The active installer and hook config docs install/register `fabric-hint.cjs` and treat `archive-hint.cjs` only as a historical name, but the fabric-archive Phase 1.5 gate reference still says E1 hook_passive comes from `archive-hint.cjs`. The integration test asserts that retired name, so tests now protect stale source text instead of current install behavior.
issues_found.fix_direction: Change E1 wording and regression expectations to `fabric-hint.cjs`; keep `archive-hint.cjs` only in explicitly historical migration notes.

issues_found.title: Store-backed pending write path conflicts with skill templates that still glob project-local `.fabric/knowledge/pending`
issues_found.description: The MCP/server path now persists and reviews pending entries under the resolved write-target store, and AGENTS says non-store knowledge roots must not be written. However fabric-review's default pending flow still tells the agent to `Glob .fabric/knowledge/pending/**/*.md`, while fabric-archive/import descriptions and guardrails still name `.fabric/knowledge/pending` as the destination. In a mounted-store project this can make review/import/archive agents look in the wrong tree or preserve obsolete protected tokens.
issues_found.fix_direction: Update skill descriptions, protected-token lists, and review backlog discovery to use `fab_review action="list"` / returned `pending_path` from the resolved write store, not direct project-local globbing.
